title: ParameterList Object
description: Transient object used to pass a set of parameters.

methods.name: add
methods.description: Adds a parameter to the list. This does not create a new parameter, it adds an existing parameter to the list. Note that duplicates can exist in the list.

methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

methods.name: contains
methods.description: Indicates whether or not ParameterList collection contains a specified parameter

methods.name: create
methods.description: Creates a parameter list that the client can use for various purposes. Use ParameterList.Add to add parameters to the list after creating it.

methods.name: find
methods.description: Finds the specified parameter in the list. The search can be started at a specified index rather than from the beginning of the list. If not found, -1 is returned.

methods.name: item
methods.description: Function that returns the specified parameter using an index into the collection.

methods.name: itemByName
methods.description: Returns the specified parameter using the name of the parameter as it is displayed in the parameters dialog

methods.name: removeByIndex
methods.description: Method that removes a parameter from the list using the index of the item in the list Will fail if the list is read only.

methods.name: removeByItem
methods.description: Method that removes a parameter from the list by specifying the parameter (item) to remove

properties.name: count
properties.description: Returns the number of parameters in the collection.

properties.name: isReadOnly
properties.description: Indicates if the list is read-only Some lists returned by API calls (instead of lists created by the user) are read only. Items cannot be added or remove from such a list.

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

related_samples.name: Create Animation API Sample
related_samples.description: Creates a series of images of a design where a parameter is being changed. The series of images can be used to create an animation using other software. To run this sample, have a part open that contains a parameter named "Length". The parameter should be able to be successfully modified from 10 to 15 centimeters. Run the sample and choose or create a directory for the output. After running you should have a folder full of images that are snapshots of each parameter value.

add.title: ParameterList.add Method
add.description: Adds a parameter to the list. This does not create a new parameter, it adds an existing parameter to the list. Note that duplicates can exist in the list.
add.return_description: Returns true if successful. This method will fail if the list is read-only

parameters.name: parameter
parameters.description: The existing parameter to add to the list

classType.title: ParameterList.classType Method
classType.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().
classType.return_description: Returns a string indicating the type of the object.

contains.title: ParameterList.contains Method
contains.description: Indicates whether or not ParameterList collection contains a specified parameter
contains.return_description: Returns true if list contains the specified parameter

parameters.name: parameter
parameters.description: The parameter to look for in the list

create.title: ParameterList.create Method
create.description: Creates a parameter list that the client can use for various purposes. Use ParameterList.Add to add parameters to the list after creating it.

find.title: ParameterList.find Method
find.description: Finds the specified parameter in the list. The search can be started at a specified index rather than from the beginning of the list. If not found, -1 is returned.

syntax.python:
# Uses no optional arguments.
returnValue = parameterList_var.find(parameter)

# Uses optional arguments.
returnValue = parameterList_var.find(parameter, startIndex)

find.return_description: Returns the index of the parameter found in the list.

parameters.name: startIndex
parameters.description: the index in the list to start the search from   This is an optional argument whose default value is 0.

item.title: ParameterList.item Method
item.description: Function that returns the specified parameter using an index into the collection.
item.return_description: Returns the specified item or null if an invalid index was specified.

parameters.name: index
parameters.description: The index of the item within the collection to return. The first item in the collection has an index of 0.

itemByName.title: ParameterList.itemByName Method
itemByName.description: Returns the specified parameter using the name of the parameter as it is displayed in the parameters dialog
itemByName.return_description: Returns the specified item or null if an invalid name was specified.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The name of the parameter as it is displayed in the parameters dialog

related_samples.name: Create Animation API Sample
related_samples.description: Creates a series of images of a design where a parameter is being changed. The series of images can be used to create an animation using other software. To run this sample, have a part open that contains a parameter named "Length". The parameter should be able to be successfully modified from 10 to 15 centimeters. Run the sample and choose or create a directory for the output. After running you should have a folder full of images that are snapshots of each parameter value.

removeByIndex.title: ParameterList.removeByIndex Method
removeByIndex.description: Method that removes a parameter from the list using the index of the item in the list Will fail if the list is read only.
removeByIndex.return_description: Returns true if successful. This method will fail if the list is read-only

parameters.name: index
parameters.description: The index of the parameter to be removed from the list

removeByItem.title: ParameterList.removeByItem Method
removeByItem.description: Method that removes a parameter from the list by specifying the parameter (item) to remove
removeByItem.return_description: Returns true if successful. This method will fail if the list is read-only

parameters.name: item
parameters.description: The parameter item to remove from the list

count.title: ParameterList.count Property
count.description: Returns the number of parameters in the collection.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = parameterList_var.count

isReadOnly.title: ParameterList.isReadOnly Property
isReadOnly.description: Indicates if the list is read-only Some lists returned by API calls (instead of lists created by the user) are read only. Items cannot be added or remove from such a list.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = parameterList_var.isReadOnly

isValid.title: ParameterList.isValid Property
isValid.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = parameterList_var.isValid

objectType.title: ParameterList.objectType Property
objectType.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object. It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = parameterList_var.objectType
